title: core
ipyfernel
text:
from solveit_modal.modal import *


app = mk_app()
img = mk_img()
sb = mk_sandbox(app, img, gpu='T4')
host,port = get_tunnel(sb)
inject_pubkey(sb, get_pubkey()); start_ssh(sb)
ssh = mk_ssh(host, port)
verify_ssh(ssh)

⌛ creating sandbox…
✔ sandbox ready
✔ tunnel: r437.modal.host:40137
✔ public key injected
✔ started ssh service
System: Linux
Hostname: modal
User: root
Kernel: 4.4.0
Architecture: x86_64
OS Type: GNU/Linux
GPU: Tesla T4




start_remote_kernel

def start_remote_kernel(
    ssh, # SSH caller from mk_ssh
    kernel_path:str='/root/.local/share/jupyter/runtime/kernel-ipyf.json', # kernel JSON path
)->None:

Start remote kernel and wait until ready.

start_remote_kernel(ssh)

✔ remote kernel ready: /root/.local/share/jupyter/runtime/kernel-ipyf.json





get_remote_python

def get_remote_python(
    ssh, # SSH caller from mk_ssh
)->str: # path to remote python

Return the path of Python on the remote machine.

p = get_remote_python(ssh); p

'/usr/local/bin/python3'



register_remote_kernel(remote_python=p)
connect_existing_kernel(f'{host}:{port}')

ipyf_remote_kernel is already a registered kernel
/app/data/.ssh/config file updated.
Successfully created connection file and forwarded ports!
Success: connected to remote kernel via r437.modal.host:40137



import platform
platform.uname()

uname_result(system='Linux', node='modal', release='4.4.0', version='#1 SMP Sun Jan 10 15:06:54 PST 2016', machine='x86_64')



!nvidia-smi

stop_remote()

Code cells will now run locally.
Shutting down remote kernel



sb.terminate()

title: modal
text:
Modal is a library that allows you to simply use cloud GPU compute programmatically in Python. No extra scripts, YAML files or whatnot needed.
Modal allows you to run your code on a GPU in multiple ways.
The third option is what solveit-modal will be using.

title: modal
text:
Additional helper functions
Additional functions to help you customize your Modal sandbox instance.


get_apts

title: gpu
text:
GPUEntry

def GPUEntry(
    tag, price, vram
):

Initialize self. See help(type(self)) for accurate signature.

GPUEntry('T4', 0.59, 16)

T4 — $0.59/hr, 16GB





GPU

def GPU(
    args:VAR_POSITIONAL, kwargs:VAR_KEYWORD
):

Initialize self. See help(type(self)) for accurate signature.

gpu = GPU(); gpu

GPU variants (as of 2026-05-22, from https://modal.com/pricing):
  A10             $1.10/hr   24GB
  A100-40GB       $2.10/hr   40GB
  A100-80GB       $2.50/hr   80GB
  B200            $6.25/hr  180GB
  H100            $3.95/hr   80GB
  H200            $4.54/hr  141GB
  L4              $0.80/hr   24GB
  L40S            $1.95/hr   48GB
  RTX-PRO-6000    $3.03/hr   96GB
  T4              $0.59/hr   16GB





GPU.cheapest

def cheapest(
    
):

Call self as a function.

gpu.cheapest()

T4 — $0.59/hr, 16GB





GPU.by_budget

def by_budget(
    max_phr:float, # GPUEntry list for GPUs costing ≤ max_phr per hour
):

Call self as a function.

gpu.by_budget(.8)

[T4 — $0.59/hr, 16GB, L4 — $0.80/hr, 24GB]





GPU.price_of

def price_of(
    tag:str, # GPUEntry for a given GPU tag
):

Call self as a function.

gpu.price_of('T4')

T4 — $0.59/hr, 16GB





GPU.tags

def tags(
    
)->list: # all available GPU tags

Call self as a function.

gpu.tags()

['T4',
 'L4',
 'A10',
 'L40S',
 'A100-40GB',
 'A100-80GB',
 'RTX-PRO-6000',
 'H100',
 'H200',
 'B200']





GPU.most_expensive

def most_expensive(
    
):

Call self as a function.

gpu.most_expensive()

B200 — $6.25/hr, 180GB





GPU.by_vram

def by_vram(
    min_gb:int, # GPUEntry list for GPUs with at least min_gb VRAM
):

Call self as a function.

gpu.by_vram(24)

[L4 — $0.80/hr, 24GB,

title: solveit-modal
text: This file will become your README and also the index of your documentation.

title: solveit-modal
text:
Developer Guide
If you are new to using nbdev here are some useful pointers to get you started.

Install solveit_modal in Development mode
# make sure solveit_modal package is installed in development mode
$ pip install -e .

# make changes under nbs/ directory
# ...

# compile to have changes apply to solveit_modal
$ nbdev_prepare

title: solveit-modal
text:
Usage

Installation
Install latest from the GitHub repository:
$ pip install git+https://github.com/ForBo7/solveit-modal.git
or from conda
$ conda install -c ForBo7 solveit_modal
or from pypi
$ pip install solveit_modal


Documentation
Documentation can be found hosted on this GitHub repository’s pages. Additionally you can find package manager specific guidelines on conda and pypi respectively.
